hypothesis: Enhancing steric complementarity at position 5 with SSTR2-unique ECL2/TM5 residues (e.g., Phe193, Val205) and introducing a hydrophobic anchor at position 11 will increase SSTR2 selectivity by differentiating from off-target SSTR3/5 pockets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that SSTR2 selectivity can be improved by disrupting conserved pocket interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 through mutations at non-pharmacophore positions while preserving the core pharmacophore. This iteration supports the hypothesis but highlights the need for more precise tuning of these positions.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved pocket contacts in SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing on non-pharmacophore positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12 will allow us to tune these interactions while preserving the pharmacophore FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14.

These mutations are proposed to disrupt conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining SSTR2 binding. For example, A1K and T2E can introduce electrostatic repulsion in off-targets, and S5R/T6D can alter hydrophobic interactions.
